Container.Name: pr-agent
Container.Overview: Self-hosted AI code reviewer (the-pr-agent/pr-agent) running as a GitHub App. Receives pull_request webhooks from GitHub, reviews diffs with an LLM (Ollama Cloud by default; Neuralwatt or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via the model prefix), and posts comments back as a distinct bot identity. No GitHub Actions runner spin-up; the webhook acks immediately and the LLM call runs on a background task with no GitHub-side timeout. Includes a /status web page showing bot identity, configured model + provider, in-flight reviews, and a live log tail. Build: (1) docker build --target github_app -t local/pr-agent:github_app -f docker/Dockerfile . (2) docker build -t local/pr-agent:github_app-status -f deploy/Dockerfile.status . (run both from the cloned pr-agent repo root on Unraid).
Container.Description: Self-hosted AI PR reviewer with a pluggable LLM backend (Ollama Cloud by default; switch to Neuralwatt or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint by changing the model prefix). Set the LLM model below (the prefix chooses the provider), point the secrets file at your filled-in .secrets.toml (provider API key + GitHub App credentials), and expose port 3000 behind a tunnel (Cloudflare Tunnel / Tailscale Funnel). Visit /status for bot status, configured provider/model, in-flight reviews, and a live log tail; /status.json for machine-readable output. See README.md in the repo for the full walkthrough and the provider list.

Config.Name: LLM model
Config.Description:
Which LLM model reviews your PRs. The PREFIX chooses the provider (pr-agent routes by prefix, not by the secrets section):
ollama/<model> → Ollama Cloud (default). Uses [ollama] in .secrets.toml. Get a key at https://ollama.com/settings/keys. Catalog: https://ollama.com/search?c=cloud. Current recommended picks (verified 2026-07-20): ollama/kimi-k2.7-code — coding-focused, lower thinking-token cost (DEFAULT, best for code review) ollama/deepseek-v4-flash — fast 1M-context MoE, good cheap fallback ollama/deepseek-v4-pro — stronger DeepSeek, three reasoning modes ollama/glm-5.2 — Z.ai flagship for long-horizon tasks ollama/kimi-k2.6 — general multimodal agentic ollama/qwen3.5:122b — largest Qwen 3.5 tag, multimodal ollama/qwen3.5:27b — mid-size Qwen, faster ollama/gpt-oss:120b — OpenAI open-weight reasoning ollama/nemotron-3-ultra — NVIDIA, long-running agents
openai/<model> → Neuralwatt or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Uses [openai] in .secrets.toml (api_base + api_key). Neuralwatt base: https://api.neuralwatt.com/v1, key from https://portal.neuralwatt.com. Live model ids (verified 2026-07-20, all OpenAI chat-completions compatible): openai/kimi-k2.7-code — same Kimi coding model, $0.95/$4.00 per M tokens openai/glm-5.2 — ZhipuAI 1M-context flagship, reasoning openai/glm-5.2-fast — non-reasoning variant of glm-5.2, same price openai/qwen3.5-397b — largest Qwen 3.5, reasoning, $0.69/$4.14 openai/qwen3.5-397b-fast — non-reasoning variant, same price openai/qwen3.6-35b — mid-size Qwen, reasoning, $0.29/$1.15 openai/kimi-k2.6 — Moonshot multimodal agentic, $0.69/$3.22 openai/gemma-4-31b — Google multimodal, $0.14/$0.42, 262K context Full Neuralwatt list (always current): curl https://api.neuralwatt.com/v1/models
⚠️ IMPORTANT: fill in ONLY ONE provider section in .secrets.toml. If both [ollama] and [openai] set an api_base, Ollama silently wins (litellm overwrites OPENAI.api_base with OLLAMA.api_base). Switching provider = change this field's prefix AND fill the matching section in .secrets.toml.
Local Ollama daemon: set to ollama/qwen2.5-coder:32b and point OLLAMA__API_BASE at http://host.docker.internal:11434 in your .secrets.toml. Other OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenRouter, Together, Groq, local vLLM/LM Studio): use openai/<model> and set [openai] api_base to the provider's /v1 URL.

Config.Name: Fallback LLM model(s)
Config.Description: Comma-separated JSON array of models tried if the primary errors or rate-limits. Use the SAME provider prefix as CONFIG__MODEL (ollama/ or openai/). Mixing prefixes works but means both [ollama] and [openai] must be set in .secrets.toml — NOT recommended (Ollama silently wins the api_base). Example: ["ollama/deepseek-v4-flash","ollama/qwen3.5:122b"] or ["openai/glm-5.2-fast","openai/qwen3.6-35b"]

Config.Name: Secrets file
Config.Description: Path to your filled-in .secrets.toml (copy secrets.example.toml from the repo and fill it in). Fill in EXACTLY ONE LLM provider section: [ollama] api_base + api_key (your https://ollama.com/settings/keys key) for Ollama Cloud, OR [openai] api_base + api_key for Neuralwatt / any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Neuralwatt api_base = https://api.neuralwatt.com/v1, key from https://portal.neuralwatt.com). Always include [github] deployment_type='app', app_id (numeric), webhook_secret, private_key (the full PEM you downloaded from the GitHub App settings). Mounted read-only. This file is the ONLY place the GitHub App private key can live — Unraid GUI fields can't hold multi-line PEM.

Config.Name: Webhook port
Config.Description: Port the webhook receiver listens on. GitHub POSTs to /api/v1/github_webhooks on this port. Put it behind your Cloudflare Tunnel / Tailscale Funnel / reverse proxy; do NOT expose it directly to the internet without TLS.

Config.Name: Model max input tokens
Config.Description: Max input tokens the model accepts. kimi-k2.7-code and deepseek-v4 support 128k+; lower this if you pick a smaller-context model. Lets pr-agent's PR-compression fit large PRs in one call.

Config.Name: AI call timeout (seconds)
Config.Description: Per-LLM-call timeout. The webhook acks GitHub in milliseconds and runs the review on a background task, so this can be generous without hitting GitHub's ~10s webhook timeout. 300s accommodates slow cloud models.

Config.Name: Max model tokens (hard cap)
Config.Description: Hard cap on tokens usable by any model, regardless of its own default. Keep aligned with CUSTOM_MODEL_MAX_TOKENS.

Config.Name: Large patch policy
Config.Description: What to do when a PR diff exceeds the token budget. 'clip' = review the first portion (always produces some review); 'skip' = skip the PR entirely.

Config.Name: Response language
Config.Description: ISO 3166 + ISO 639 locale for review comments. e.g. en-US, de-DE, fr-FR, zh-CN. Change to de-DE for German reviews.

Config.Name: Repo context files
Config.Description: JSON array of repo-relative files (e.g. AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) to inject into review prompts. Default [] = none — keeps reviews focused on the diff and avoids prompt bloat from repo-level agent instructions.

Config.Name: Gunicorn workers
Config.Description: Number of gunicorn worker processes. Keep at 1 so the /status page's in-memory log buffer sees ALL webhook activity (each worker has its own buffer). The webhook acks in milliseconds and reviews run as background tasks, so one worker handles concurrent reviews fine for a personal bot. Raise only if you review many PRs simultaneously (and accept that /status will show only one worker's logs).

Config.Name: Log level
Config.Description: Log verbosity: INFO (normal) or DEBUG (verbose, for troubleshooting).

Config.Name: Auto-review on PR actions
Config.Description: JSON array of pull_request actions that trigger auto-review. Defaults: opened, reopened, ready_for_review, synchronize (new commits). Remove 'synchronize' if you don't want re-reviews on every push.

Config.Name: Auto-run tools
Config.Description: JSON array of pr-agent commands run automatically on each new PR. /describe = AI PR description, /review = inline code review, /improve = committable code suggestions. Remove any you don't want. Available: /describe, /review, /improve, /ask, /update_changelog, /add_docs, /analyze.

Config.Name: Re-review on new commits
Config.Description: When true, re-run /review automatically when new commits land on an open PR (synchronize action).

Config.Name: Re-review tools
Config.Description: JSON array of commands run on synchronize (new commits). Defaults to /describe + /review (skips /improve to save cost on every push).

Config.Name: Review: require tests review
Config.Description: When true, the reviewer flags missing tests for new behavior. Noisy on pure source changes, so off by default. Toggle on for repos where test coverage matters.

Config.Name: Review: require security review
Config.Description: Run the security sub-review (injection, auth gaps, secret leakage, etc.). Recommended on.

Config.Name: Review: max findings
Config.Description: Cap on the number of findings reported per review. Lower = less noisy, higher = more thorough.

Config.Name: Improve: score threshold
Config.Description: 0-10. /improve only surfaces suggestions scoring at least this high. 7 = only real problems. Lower (4-5) for more suggestions, raise (8-9) for stricter.

Config.Name: Improve: suggestions per chunk
Config.Description: Number of code suggestions per diff chunk in /improve. Lower = faster, less noisy.
